ground_truth.title: 2024.4.4 -> 2024.5.0 issue: stuck on "Home Assistant is starting"
ground_truth.author: raoul
ground_truth.main_content:
raoul
May 3, 2024, 8:45am

2024.4.4 -> 2024.5.0 issue
I have just installed version 2024.5.0, but at every reboot attempt the message "Home Assistant is starting, not everything will be available until it is finished" remains visible and I have verified that several devices are not available. I then went back to version 2024.4.4 and with this everything works fine. I tried to put the registry here, but it turns out to be too large. How can I do? Could you help me solve the problem? Thank you.

raoul
May 3, 2024, 11:39am

This is part of the log, I hope it is sufficient:

2024-05-03 10:09:38.163 ERROR (MainThread) [homeassistant] Error doing job: Future exception was never retrieved
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/concurrent/futures/thread.py", line 58, in run
    result = self.fn(*self.args, **self.kwargs)
  File "/config/custom_components/tuya_local/device.py", line 147, in actually_start
    self._refresh_task = self._hass.async_create_task(self.receive_loop())
  File "/usr/src/homeassistant/homeassistant/core.py", line 816, in async_create_task
    self.verify_event_loop_thread("async_create_task")
  File "/usr/src/homeassistant/homeassistant/core.py", line 440, in verify_event_loop_thread
    frame.report(
  File "/usr/src/homeassistant/homeassistant/helpers/frame.py", line 162, in report
    _report_integration(what, integration_frame, level, error_if_integration)
  File "/usr/src/homeassistant/homeassistant/helpers/frame.py", line 203, in _report_integration
    raise RuntimeError(
RuntimeError: Detected that custom integration 'tuya_local' calls async_create_task from a thread at custom_components/tuya_local/device.py, line 147: self._refresh_task = self._hass.async_create_task(self.receive_loop()). Please report it to the author of the 'tuya_local' custom integration.

code-in-progress (Bill - I write code.)
May 3, 2024, 12:16pm

Looks like it's something to do with the tuya_local integration. Try disabling it and restarting.

You might also want to open an issue on the custom integration's github as well.

raoul
May 3, 2024, 1:23pm

I found a thread already open on GitHub:

Last update don't solve my problem with HA 2024.5.0 stops the integration

It seems to be a general problem and is being discussed.
Thanks for the suggestion.
with: I have just installed version 2024.5.0, but at every reboot attempt the message "Home Assistant is starting, not everything will be available until it is finished" remains visible
I then went back to version 2024.4.4 and with this everything works fine.

Looks like it's something to do with the tuya_local integration. Try disabling it and restarting.
You might also want to open an issue on the custom integration's github as well.
